Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board didn't change (board_changed: false), which means the player couldn't move LEFT. The player is blocked by the left frame. Let me check the segmentation to see the current state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Player position:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\nFrames:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check adjacency
print("\nAdjacency list:")
for adj in seg['adjacency_list']:
    i, j = adj
    ni = next((n for n in nodes if n['id'] == i), None)
    nj = next((n for n in nodes if n['id'] == j), None)
    if ni and nj:
        print(f"  {ni['color']}@{ni['boundary']} <-> {nj['color']}@{nj['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player is at col 20-23, and the left frame is at col 16-19. The player's white head (id=1) is adjacent to the left frame (id=3). The player's green body (id=6) is also adjacent to the left frame.
But the left frame is still 'c', not 'G'. This is different from the right frame, which became 'G' when the player was adjacent.
Wait, looking at the adjacency list, the player's white head (W) is adjacent to the left frame (c). But the frame is still 'c'.
Hmm, maybe the frame activation depends on the player being in the same column range, not just adjacent. For the right frame, the player was at col 44-47, and the frame was at col 44-47 (same columns). For the left frame, the player is at col 20-23, and the frame is at col 16-19 (different columns).
So I need to move the player to be in the same column range as the left frame (col 16-19). But the player is blocked from moving LEFT.
Wait, looking at the adjacency list, the player's green body (N) is adjacent to the left frame (c). This means the player is touching the frame. But the frame is still 'c'.
Let me think about this differently. For the right frame, the player was at row 28-31, col 44-47 (below the frame at row 24-27, col 44-47). The frame became 'G' when the player was in the same column range.
For the left frame, the player is at row 28-31, col 20-23 (to the right of the frame at row 28-31, col 16-19). The frame is in the same row range but different column range.
Maybe the frame only activates when the player is above or below it (same column range), not to the left or right.
Let me try moving UP to get above the left frame, then move LEFT to be in the same column range, then move DOWN to be below it.
Actually, the left frame is at row 28-31, col 16-19. The player is at row 28-31 (green body), col 20-23. To be in the same column range, the player needs to be at col 16-19. But the player is blocked from moving LEFT.
Let me try moving UP to get above the left frame, then move LEFT to be above the frame, then move DOWN to be below the frame.
Actually, wait. The player's white head is at row 24-28, col 20-23. The left frame is at row 28-31, col 16-19. The white head's bottom edge is at row 28, which is the same as the frame's top edge. So the white head is adjacent to the frame.
But the frame is still 'c'. Maybe the frame only activates when the player is directly above or below it (same column range), not to the side.
Let me try moving UP to get above the left frame, then move LEFT to be above the frame (same column range), then move DOWN to be below the frame.
Actually, I think the issue is that the player can't move LEFT because the frame is blocking. Let me try moving UP first to get above the frame, then move LEFT.

Goal model: Collect all three frames. Right frame collected. Need to activate and collect left and center-below frames.
Plan: Move UP to get above the left frame, then move LEFT to be above the frame (same column range), then move DOWN to be below the frame.
